cases.brief:
# Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

**When:** Wed 1 Jul 2026 · **Who:** Greg Sullivan, VP Operations (economic buyer); CTO Dana Cole may weigh in · **Purpose:** Reliability follow-up ahead of 31 Aug renewal · **Sources:** Verizon (account), QBR + Outage post-mortem + Reliability plan review (meetings), Greg Sullivan + Dana Cole (contacts) · Web: https://news.example.com/verizon-frontier-acquisition

---

## Bottom line
Verizon ($980K ARR) is **At Risk** and renews 31 August. Two Q2 outages badly damaged trust, and Greg is openly evaluating alternatives with High competitor threat. This meeting is won or lost on one thing: showing measurable uptime improvement with hard data and a credible plan — no excuses.

## Who you're meeting
- **Greg Sullivan — VP Operations, economic buyer.** Blunt, ops-focused, impatient. Measures everything in uptime and tickets; low tolerance for excuses.
- **Dana Cole — CTO, decision-maker (Greg's boss).** Makes the stay-or-switch call; less emotional than Greg but wants the reliability data.

## What's changed since you last spoke
- Two Q2 outages hurt trust; Greg requested a formal reliability improvement plan (post-mortem 8 May, plan review 8 Jun — both negative sentiment).
- Sentiment has slid QBR → escalation → check-in; he's now openly weighing competitors.
- Company-level: Verizon agreed to acquire Frontier's fiber business for $9.6B — a broadband-expansion move that raises the stakes on partner reliability.

## Likely to come up
- Proof the reliability plan is working — uptime numbers since the last review.
- Whether outages can recur; what's structurally changed in monitoring.
- Renewal terms and the case to stay versus switch.

## Your goals & talking points
- **Lead with data:** present uptime gains since 8 Jun, framed in his language (uptime, tickets).
- **Own the plan:** walk through concrete monitoring/reliability changes already shipped.
- **Protect the renewal:** rebuild confidence now; surface any blockers before August.

## Watch-outs
- No excuses or hand-waving — bring evidence, not explanations.
- Don't over-promise fixes you can't measure; he'll hold you to every number.

## Desired outcome
Greg leaves convinced reliability is genuinely fixed and willing to advance toward an on-time August renewal.

checks: # Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

judge.grounding_reason: Every claim traces to context: $980K ARR, At Risk, 31 Aug renewal, two Q2 outages, Greg openly evaluating, High competitor threat, both contacts' roles/styles, meeting dates and sentiments (8 May post-mortem negative, 8 Jun check-in negative, QBR), 1 Jul next meeting, and the $9.6B Frontier acquisition from the web source. Sources line cites only real pages.
judge.correctness_reason: Accurately captures the at-risk reliability situation: data-driven uptime proof, no excuses, the stay-or-switch dynamic with Dana Cole as decision-maker, and renewal protection ahead of 31 Aug. Web M&A item correctly kept company-level. Useful and on-point.
judge.tone_reason: Plain, direct, confident; uses the contact's own uptime/tickets framing, no filler or flattery.

cases.brief:
# Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

**When:** Wed 1 Jul 2026 · **Who:** Greg Sullivan, VP Operations (economic buyer) · **Purpose:** Reliability plan review to defend an at-risk renewal · **Sources:** Verizon (account), QBR + Outage post-mortem + Reliability plan review (meetings), Greg Sullivan + Dana Cole (contacts)

---

## Bottom line
Verizon ($980K ARR) is **at risk** and renews **31 Aug** — two months out. Two Q2 outages badly damaged trust, and Greg is openly evaluating alternatives with a High competitor threat. This meeting is won or lost on data: bring measurable uptime improvement and a credible reliability plan, not explanations.

## Who you're meeting
- **Greg Sullivan** — VP Operations, economic buyer. Blunt, ops-focused, impatient; measures everything in uptime and tickets, low tolerance for excuses.
- **Dana Cole** — CTO, Greg's boss and the stay-or-switch decision-maker. Less emotional than Greg but wants the reliability data. (May not attend, but is the audience behind Greg.)

## What's changed since you last spoke
- Two outages in Q2 hurt trust badly; sentiment has gone from Neutral (Mar QBR) to Negative.
- After the 8 May post-mortem, Greg requested a **formal reliability improvement plan**.
- At the 8 Jun check-in he was openly evaluating alternatives and demanding measurable uptime gains.

## Likely to come up
- Hard uptime numbers since the outages — proof the trend has turned.
- The specifics and timeline of the reliability improvement plan.
- Renewal terms and whether reliability justifies the current price.

## Your goals & talking points
- **Lead with data:** open with concrete post-outage uptime and ticket metrics — no preamble, no excuses.
- **Present the plan:** walk through the formal reliability plan with owners and dates Greg can hold you to.
- **Stabilize the renewal:** reconnect reliability gains to renewal value; signal commitment without negotiating price on the spot.

## Watch-outs
- Don't make excuses for the outages — Greg will read it as evasion.
- Don't over-promise uptime you can't evidence; he measures everything.
- Don't commit to discounts here; this meeting is about trust, not terms.

## Desired outcome
Greg accepts the reliability plan as sufficient to pause his evaluation and re-engage on renewal.

checks: # Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

judge.grounding_reason: Every specific claim traces to the fetched context: $980K ARR, At Risk status, 31 Aug renewal, two Q2 outages, High competitor threat, Greg Sullivan (VP Ops, economic buyer), Dana Cole (CTO, Greg's boss, decision-maker), sentiment Neutral at Mar QBR shifting to Negative, 8 May post-mortem requesting a formal reliability plan, 8 Jun check-in with open evaluation of alternatives, and the 1 Jul meeting date. Sources line cites real pages present in context. Dana 'may not attend' is correctly hedged (meeting attendees list only Greg) rather than asserted.
judge.correctness_reason: Accurately captures the account's real situation: an at-risk renewal endangered by reliability failures, a blunt data-driven buyer, and a CTO decision-maker behind him. Talking points (lead with uptime data, present the plan, don't make excuses or discount) map directly to the meeting notes' next steps and watch-outs. 'Two months out' is correct for a 28 Jun→31 Aug horizon.
judge.tone_reason: Plain, direct, confident; no filler or flattery. Imperative talking points and clear watch-outs match the blunt ops-focused style implied by the context.

cases.brief:
# Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

**When:** Wed 1 Jul 2026 · **Who:** Greg Sullivan, VP Operations (economic buyer) · **Purpose:** Reliability/churn-risk check-in ahead of the 31 Aug renewal · **Sources:** Verizon (account), QBR + Outage post-mortem + Reliability plan review (meetings), Greg Sullivan + Dana Cole (contacts)

---

## Bottom line
Verizon ($980K ARR) is At Risk and up for renewal on 31 Aug, with a High competitor threat. Two Q2 outages badly damaged trust and Greg is openly evaluating alternatives. This meeting is about one thing: showing measurable uptime improvement with hard data and a credible plan — not promises or excuses.

## Who you're meeting
- **Greg Sullivan** — VP Operations, economic buyer. Blunt, ops-focused, impatient; measures everything in uptime and tickets, low tolerance for excuses.
- **Dana Cole** — CTO and Greg's boss, makes the stay-or-switch call. Less emotional than Greg but wants the reliability data. (May not attend; influence the room with her in mind.)

## What's changed since you last spoke
- Two outages in Q2 hurt trust badly; the 8 May post-mortem closed Negative.
- Greg requested a formal reliability improvement plan; the 8 Jun review stayed Negative with him weighing alternatives.
- Trajectory has slid from Neutral (Mar QBR) to Negative across both Q2 touchpoints.

## Likely to come up
- Proof that uptime has actually improved since the outages — numbers, not narrative.
- Status and specifics of the reliability improvement plan.
- Whether to renew, and on what terms, given competitor pressure.

## Your goals & talking points
- **Lead with data:** open with concrete uptime/ticket metrics showing the trend reversing.
- **Own the plan:** walk through the formal reliability plan with owners and dates; tie each fix to a past failure.
- **Stabilize the relationship:** acknowledge the damage plainly, then pivot to forward commitments that de-risk renewal.

## Watch-outs
- No excuses — Greg will read hedging as weakness. Bring evidence or don't raise it.
- Don't over-promise on timelines you can't hit; a missed commitment now likely loses the renewal.

## Desired outcome
Greg leaves convinced reliability is fixed and trending right, and agrees to advance the renewal rather than shop alternatives.

checks: # Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

judge.grounding_reason: Every factual claim traces to the fetched context: $980K ARR, At Risk, 31 Aug renewal, High competitor threat, two Q2 outages, Greg openly evaluating alternatives, Greg Sullivan (VP Ops, economic buyer), Dana Cole (CTO, decision-maker, Greg's boss), meeting dates (Mar QBR neutral, 8 May post-mortem negative, 8 Jun review negative). Sources line cites real pages. The unrelated Verizon Cafe web item was correctly ignored. 'May not attend' for Dana is a flagged hypothesis, not an asserted fact.
judge.correctness_reason: Accurately captures the account's real situation: churn/reliability risk, trust damage from outages, demand for measurable uptime data and a credible plan, and renewal stakes under competitor pressure. Sentiment trajectory and next-steps map correctly to the meetings. Talking points and watch-outs align with the contact style and watch-out notes.
judge.tone_reason: Plain, direct, confident. No filler or flattery; bottom line is crisp and action-oriented, matching Greg's blunt, data-first style.

cases.brief:
# Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

**When:** Wed 1 Jul 2026 · **Who:** Greg Sullivan, VP Operations (economic buyer); likely Dana Cole, CTO (decision-maker) · **Purpose:** Reliability plan review ahead of 31 Aug renewal · **Sources:** Verizon (account), QBR + Outage post-mortem + Reliability plan review (meetings), Greg Sullivan + Dana Cole (contacts) · Web: https://news.example.com/verizon-vp-network-ops

---

## Bottom line
Verizon ($980K ARR) is At Risk and up for renewal 31 August. Two Q2 outages badly damaged trust, and Greg is openly evaluating alternatives with a High competitor threat. This meeting is about one thing: showing measurable uptime improvement with hard data and a credible reliability plan — no excuses.

## Who you're meeting
- **Greg Sullivan** — VP Operations, economic buyer. Blunt, ops-focused, impatient; measures everything in uptime and tickets. Low tolerance for excuses.
- **Dana Cole** — CTO, Greg's boss and the decision-maker on stay-or-switch. Less emotional than Greg, but wants the reliability data.

## What's changed since you last spoke
- Two outages in Q2 severely hurt trust; Greg formally requested a reliability improvement plan (post-mortem, 8 May).
- At the 8 Jun check-in, sentiment was negative — Greg openly evaluating alternatives and demanding measurable uptime gains.
- Verizon named Jordan Vance as new VP of Network Operations — a possible new technical stakeholder (news.example.com).

## Likely to come up
- Concrete uptime numbers since the last outage and proof the fixes hold.
- The formal reliability improvement plan and its milestones.
- Renewal terms and whether reliability justifies staying.

## Your goals & talking points
- **Lead with data:** open with uptime metrics and ticket trends, not narrative — that's how Greg measures success.
- **Present the plan:** walk through the reliability improvement plan with clear owners and dates.
- **Stabilize the renewal:** reframe the relationship around demonstrated improvement ahead of 31 Aug.

## Watch-outs
- No excuses or hedging — show data and a plan only.
- Don't over-promise fixes you can't measure; Greg will hold you to every number.

## Desired outcome
Greg and Dana accept the reliability plan as credible and agree to proceed toward renewal.

checks: # Meeting Brief — Verizon (Greg Sullivan, VP Operations)

judge.grounding_reason: Every factual claim traces to the fetched context: $980K ARR, At Risk, 31 Aug renewal, High competitor threat, two Q2 outages, Greg Sullivan (VP Ops, economic buyer), Dana Cole (CTO, decision-maker), 8 May post-mortem, 8 Jun negative check-in, all three meetings, and Wed 1 Jul date (2026-07-01 is indeed a Wednesday). judge.correctness_reason: Accurately captures the account's real situation — churn/reliability risk, the data-and-plan-no-excuses imperative driven by Greg's style, the renewal stakes, and Dana as the stay-or-switch decision-maker. Talking points and watch-outs are well-aligned with the post-mortem and reliability-plan-review next steps.
judge.tone_reason: Plain, direct, confident; no flattery or filler. 'No excuses', 'lead with data' mirror the source's blunt ops framing without overstating.
